text.title: GCP Terraform Templates Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules plus a skip-clause for `GCP Terraform Templates`. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every root MUST pin `google` and `google-beta` provider versions with `~> MAJOR.MINOR` syntax; floating `latest` is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Unpinned providers introduce silent drift on the next `terraform init`; reproducible builds require pinning.

rule.statement: State MUST live in a remote GCS backend with object versioning enabled; local state files are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Concurrent applies corrupt local state; versioning provides recovery from accidental destroys.

rule.statement: Production resources (Cloud SQL, GKE clusters, KMS keys) MUST set `deletion_protection = true`; modules expose it as a required input with default `true`.

rationale.source: GCP deletion protection docs, internal incident retro

rule.rationale: Accidental `terraform destroy` is the #1 root cause of data loss; deletion_protection blocks the API.

rule.statement: Module `source` MUST be a versioned reference (`git::https://...?ref=v1.2.3` or registry `version = "~> 1.2"`); `main`/`HEAD` references are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Floating refs cause non-reproducible plans; version pins enable safe rollback.

rule.statement: CI must authenticate to GCP via Workload Identity Federation; long-lived SA JSON keys are forbidden in CI secrets.
rule.rationale: Static keys are the dominant GCP credential-leak vector; WIF removes the surface entirely.

rule.statement: If `Applies If` preconditions fail OR any `Skip If` trigger fires, do not run this methodology.
